name: Peanut Stuffed Chicken Breast
recipeIngredient: 1 Package G. Washington's Golden Bouillon Mix
recipeInstructions:
1. Bone the chicken breasts if needed, keeping the meat as intact as possible.
It is easier to rip the meat off the bones than any knife work.
2. Pull off shreds and pieces of the breast meat.
3. In a bowl, mix together peanut butter, honey, cumin, and dried mint until well combined.
4. Microwave for 30 seconds or so to thin it out and make mixing easier.
5. Spread about 2 teaspoons of the peanut butter mixture over each piece of chicken breast meat.
6. Place an equal amount of shredded chicken on top of the peanut butter spread on each breast.
7. Roll up the chicken breasts tightly into packets.
8. Tie the packets shut with butcher's twine or secure with a toothpick.
9. Arrange the twelve packets in layers in the crock pot, ensuring they do not fall open.
10. In a bowl, stir water into any remaining peanut butter mixture and add the bouillon packet.
11. Pour over the chicken packets, adding water as needed to cover them by at least one inch.
12. Cook on high for 5-6 hours.
13. Remove the packets from the pot and let drain on a platter. The chicken fat will have migrated to the top of the liquid, which can be used for flavoring in soups and other dishes.
